item.title: The streets they are a'changin'

item.title: A friend asked me to talk him off a ledge (via text). I thought he was joking, so I sent memes. This one got him to call me.
item.description: After a long conversation, he's seeking help and I'm hopeful for him. IDK who the original source is, and I just saw it for the first time today, but you may have saved a couple of lives. No hyperbole. Edit: Tubby Nugget. https://www.tubbynugget.com/ Thanks @Randomice
item.dc:creator: Yakeshinu

item.title: I lost my wife yesterday to heart failure...
item.description:
This was us 15 years ago.
Halloween 2013 she was Black Widow to my Hawkeye
Our kitties loved sleeping on her for some reason?
Need I say more?
Kitten tax!! These little ones would visit us at our cabin in Cloudcroft NM. It all started over 18 months or so with a diagnosis of heart failure stage 2. It progressed pretty slowly until about 5 months ago when she couldn't walk anymore. Then it became a spiral downward of not being able to eat more than 2 bites of any food without getting nauseous or vomiting. Then the weakness from not eating and losing a terrible amount of body weight. I tried to get her to eat by getting her anything she wanted. If she saw a nice burger or pizza commercial she would say "I'd love to eat one of those" and I would go get it for her. But again 2-3 bites and she felt sick again. We had a great life together and she will be missed by our daughters and myself terribly.
item.dc:creator: GIMike1972

item.title: Great moment between a father and a son.
item.dc:creator: Nomaketuti

item.title: Tesla Fans Furious at Video of Tesla Crashing Into Wall Painted Like Road ... LIDAR vs. Camera Test ...
item.description:
Explanation of technology.
Both work fine for "Kid in Road" and "Kid Chasing Ball into Street."
Add thick fog ...
Wall of water ... Both pass the super bright light test ...
And the FINAL - a painted "Road Runner" wall ...
Basically in the mid terms, Tesla is 3 for 5 (60%, not a great grade), CAMERA is 5 for 5 (100%). And the FINAL? Tesla camera technology - fail. LIDAR wins easily. Tesla has already been extremely reluctant about handing over crash data, especially when it comes to its infamous "Full Self-Driving" feature, duking it out with the California Department of Motor Vehicles in 2023. Tesla has also gone after crash data collected by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). The EV maker reportedly asked the regulator to redact information about whether its Autopilot or FSD software was used during every single documented crash since 2021. In short, Rober's latest video still points out a glaring shortcoming when it comes to Tesla's safety features — no matter how unhappy it makes Tesla fanboys. https://futurism.com/tesla-fans-furious-video-tesla-crashing-wall Full Original Video HERE: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IQJL3htsDyQ&t (The first 9 minutes is him going to Disneyland to stealthily film a ride on Space Mountain to map it on LIDAR (spoiler alert: It's a roller coaster indoors).
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: Support for people who menstruate from British retailer Aldi
item.description: https://www.thegrocer.co.uk/news/aldi-providing-free-period-products-in-stores-as-of-today/702317.article Couldn't find a great source (the above paywalled me after the first view) but there's all the information from the Aldi website here: https://www.aldi.co.uk/period-poverty We love the gender inclusive language they used; because it's not just women and girls who get their periods. Trans people exist and it's nice that Aldi gets it. **I thought ALDI was British and I have been enlightened - it's German. Entschuldigung a thousand times.
item.dc:creator: TheReaIestAIpharius

item.title: Rosie
item.description: Our little (2.35kg) baby girl passed just shy of her 8th birthday this year. She had developed an aggressive oral cancer and she couldn't eat anymore. She was our Disney cat because this is how she looked all the time. I figured I should share so her cuteness could help make people's day a little better.
item.dc:creator: TuroktheXmasDestroyer

item.title: A Pangolin reacts happily to a kind man grooming it in places it could not reach or take care of it by itself.

item.title: Functioning.....Just Dysfunctional
item.description: If you're looking for silly, strange, and playful stories/comics among other stuff too, come check out my stories here on Imgur. Who knows. It might make for a decent distraction. Knowing I can be some good part of a person's day is one of the few joys I have. https://imgur.com/user/shanefrost/posts And if you're feeling especially generous, here's a link to my patreon! Bless you silly dears! https://www.patreon.com/join/Shane_Frost
item.dc:creator: shanefrost

item.title: What Happened Today #51
item.description: Trump’s crackdown is accelerating. Lawyers punished. Judges mocked. Kids in court alone. ICE may get IRS data. FEMA gutted. A Cornell Ph.D. student targeted for suing. All of this on a Saturday. But across the West, the “Fighting Oligarchy” tour is drawing thousands. AOC and Bernie Sanders showed up. 30,000 in Denver. Thousands more in Phoenix, Vegas, and Tucson. They’re not waiting. They’re leading. Follow them. ?️ Start local at fiftyfifty.one ? Show up. Push back. ? Subscribe free → govbrief.today ? Share this. Post it. Organize. #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/politics/irs-nears-deal-with-ice-to-share-addresses-of-suspected-undocumented-immigrants/ar-AA1BsLgW 2. https://thehill.com/homenews/administration/5208866-donald-trump-justice-department-law-firms-government-litigation/ 3. https://www.upi.com/Top_News/US/2025/03/22/Pentagon-leaks-national-security-polygraph-tests/9521742667076/ 4. https://pbswisconsin.org/news-item/trump-endorses-schimel-in-the-2025-race-for-a-wisconsin-supreme-court-seat/ 5. https://www.wusf.org/politics-issues/2025-03-22/fort-myers-council-reverse-course-allow-ice-train-police-officers 6. https://apnews.com/article/trump-legal-aid-unaccompanied-children-immigration-court-127a69ce69573d2d16c72a74dacef3ab 7. https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/wireStory/researchers-us-government-erase-sexual-orientation-findings-120041183 8. https://www.cbsnews.com/news/venezuela-reaches-deal-to-accept-deportation-flights-from-u-s/ 9. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/03/21/trump-air-craft-carrier-red-sea-00243745 10. https://www.wsj.com/economy/central-banking/federal-reserve-posted-loss-of-77-6-billion-in-2024-d06a2101? 11. https://www.cbsnews.com/news/fema-hiring-overhaul-fear-agency-gutting-hurricane-season-trump/ 12. https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-03-22/white-house-energy-adviser-departs-as-trump-reorders-us-agenda?srnd=phx-politics 13. https://www.bangordailynews.com/2025/03/22/politics/state-politics/trump-wants-full-throated-apology-from-mills/ 14. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/mar/23/trump-news-at-a-glance-hegseth-mocks-judge-who-ruled-against-transgender-ban 15. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/momodou-taal-cornell-student-activist-surrender-ice-rcna197604 16. https://tucson.com/news/local/government-politics/elections/article_5d542f0a-0366-11f0-a0ee-d340c8967465.html
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Michigan Native Pulls Of Fabeled "Michigan Goal"
item.description:
New York Sirens forward Abby Roque became the first player in Professional Women's Hockey League history to score a "Michigan goal," using the fabled trick shot against the Ottawa Charge on Saturday afternoon. "The Michigan" first came to hockey prominence in 1996, when Mike Legg of the University of Michigan pulled off a "lacrosse-style" goal in an NCAA playoff game. Starting in back of the opponent's net, a skater scoops the puck onto the blade of their stick, lifts it up and then whips a shot around the net into the top corner, over the shoulder of an unsuspecting goaltender. https://www.espn.com/espn/story/_/id/44360791/pwhl-abby-roque-pulls-fabled-michigan-goal
Abby Roque (born September 25, 1997) is an American ice hockey forward for the New York Sirens of the Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL). She is the first indigenous person to play for the United States women's national ice hockey team, making her Olympic debut in 2022.
item.dc:creator: UnattendedDeviant
